content.text: The reviewer's point is valid: `onInput` triggers `onLog` (a DB write) on every keystroke, and the `.trim()` in `setNotes` strips trailing spaces as the user types — so a user typing "good set " can't keep that trailing space mid-word. Confirmed this is Preact (`onChange` fires on blur natively, not per-keystroke). Applying the suggested fix.

git add src/tabs/Workouts/components/ExerciseCard.tsx && git commit -m "workouts: use onChange for notes textarea to avoid per-keystroke writes

onInput fired onLog (a DB write) on every keystroke, and the .trim() in
setNotes stripped trailing spaces as the user typed — preventing a
trailing space mid-word. In Preact, onChange fires on the native change
event (on blur), so the write happens once when editing finishes." && git push

content.text: Pushed. Now replying to the review comment.

input.command: gh api repos/gunk-dev/flux/pulls/82/comments/3280634978/replies -f body='Good catch — switched to `onChange` so the DB write only happens when the textarea loses focus, which also lets the user type trailing spaces mid-word without `.trim()` stripping them.'

content.text:
Done. Summary:

- **One review comment** from gemini-code-assist flagged `onInput` on the notes textarea (`src/tabs/Workouts/components/ExerciseCard.tsx:305`) as inefficient (DB write per keystroke) and buggy (`.trim()` strips trailing spaces during typing).
- **Fix:** Switched to `onChange`. In Preact, `onChange` maps to the native `change` event, which fires on blur — so `setNotes`/`onLog` runs once when editing finishes, and trailing spaces mid-edit are preserved.
- **Verified:** typecheck passes; all 15 Playwright tests pass.
- Commit `5f93815` pushed; reply posted to the review comment.

result:
Done. Summary:

- **One review comment** from gemini-code-assist flagged `onInput` on the notes textarea (`src/tabs/Workouts/components/ExerciseCard.tsx:305`) as inefficient (DB write per keystroke) and buggy (`.trim()` strips trailing spaces during typing).
- **Fix:** Switched to `onChange`. In Preact, `onChange` maps to the native `change` event, which fires on blur — so `setNotes`/`onLog` runs once when editing finishes, and trailing spaces mid-edit are preserved.
- **Verified:** typecheck passes; all 15 Playwright tests pass.
- Commit `5f93815` pushed; reply posted to the review comment.
